title: Piquant Crispy Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A floral and crispy Algerian dish featuring lamb and five spice powder, cooked on a cedar plank, this recipe is gluten free friendly and perfect for a special occasion, the nimono simmer method adds a light and delicate touch to the dish.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into 1 inch pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the five spice powder, salt, and pepper.
Add the spice mixture to the lamb pieces and toss to coat.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add the lamb to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped apricots, prunes, chicken broth, and honey to the skillet and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Place the cedar plank in the oven and heat for 5 minutes.
Remove the plank from the oven and place the lamb mixture on the plank.
Return the plank to the oven and cook for 15 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked through and the top is crispy.
